sources_audited.note: The 2022 NAICS definition supports the limited-service ordering and payment model, consumption and delivery channels, and exclusion of independent order-and-delivery services.
The May 2023 BLS NAICS 722500 table reports the cited total employment and occupation shares; the broader-than-722513 population is accurately disclosed.
O*NET lists the cited mix of order taking, payment, inventory, preparation, packaging, serving, stocking, cleaning, and customer-communication tasks.
BLS describes cooks' physical and sanitation duties and projects fast-food-cook employment to decline 13% during 2024-2034, citing automated systems, combined roles, and streamlining.
The 2024 National Restaurant Association report contains the cited limited-service investment percentages and the 49%, 69%, and 3% labor-automation, augmentation, and robotics figures.
Deloitte reports a Q4 2024 survey of 375 restaurant executives in 11 countries, the cited 82% investment intention, and readiness percentages across the five dimensions.
Census reports that 2012 limited-service franchise restaurants represented approximately 54% of establishments, nearly 70% of sales, and about 73% of employment.

Project Equity's compilation reports 49% planning an exit within five years, 17% preferring external sales, and one-third lacking or being unsure of a long-term plan, with the underlying cross-industry nature visible.
BizBuySell reports 2,516 restaurant transactions in 2025 versus 2,653 in 2024 and the cited median revenue, cash flow, and sale price.
The BLS 2024-2034 table reports food-services-and-drinking-places output of $994.2 billion and $1,230.1 billion, a 2.2% compound annual rate, and 3.8% employment growth.

The live USDA page has been revised to 2025 data and still supports convenience-led outlet expansion, recurring seasonal demand, and a large limited-service share, but it no longer displays the packet's exact $550.7 billion and 36.3% 2024 figures; an indexed prior version supports those values, subject to small later revisions.

The National Restaurant Association page reports 1.3% expected real sales growth in 2026, 42% of operators unprofitable in 2025 with limited menu-pricing ability, and 61% of consumers viewing restaurants as essential while responding to value offers and loyalty programs.

caveats: The a range is an openly labeled proxy built from a broader NAICS 722500 occupation mix and qualitative task mapping; no cited source supplies the wage-weighted task-time calculation needed to validate the 0.30 base directly.

The rho range translates technology spending intentions and large/global operator readiness into realized five-year labor implementation for LMM operators; deployment, sustained use, and labor substitution are not observed.

The e and s5 bases are weakly anchored for the stated EBITDA band: franchise establishment shares do not measure eligible firms, and cross-industry exit intentions plus small marketplace deals do not identify the five-year qualifying-control-transfer rate.

The q and o primitives are honest estimates rather than direct measurements; cited pricing pressure and task evidence establish direction but do not identify five-year benefit retention or operator-required quantity shares.

The d5 bridge relies on broader NAICS 722 output and restaurant-wide forecasts rather than 722513 transaction quantities, and the live USDA S11 page has replaced the cited 2024 snapshot with revised 2025 data.

The injected firm count is an ESTIMATE derived from coarse employment-size classes and a public-company restaurant margin, so the saturated F factor may overstate target availability even though that weakness is disclosed and does not drive the base tier.
summary: Mechanics pass and all 12 primitive-cited sources were reachable. The record is useful and internally coherent: evidence states are candid, proxy bridges disclose their population and transformation limits, construct boundaries are respected, and the LOW_PRIORITY base tier is driven by the weak H factor rather than unsupported favorable claims. The main weaknesses are broad-population proxies, unobserved task-time and implementation mappings, estimate-heavy transfer and retention primitives, the uncertain injected firm denominator, and a revised USDA page; none meets the methodology's materiality test because an honest evidence-state downgrade or the supported correction identified here would change neither the LOW_PRIORITY base tier nor the VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action.
